name: swarm-daemon-acceptance
verifier: independent clone + probes against SQLite ground truth; every check is pass/fail, no partial credit

S1_event_loop.check: daemon start with valid token env connects Socket Mode and survives a forced disconnect (reconnect within 30s, logged)
message posted in connected channel appears as durable swarm message + mapping row without any manual export/ingest command
Slack redelivery of the same event id creates no duplicate rows
daemon start with missing token env exits nonzero with SLACK_TOKEN_MISSING; unknown flags rejected INVALID_ARG

S2_wake_router.check: message containing @<agent> wakes exactly that registered agent; unregistered names wake nobody and log a routing miss
two events for one agent within one turn produce one running turn + one pending-wake marker; second turn starts only after first ends
due reminder wakes its author agent through the same router path

S3_turn_runner.check: turn spawns the registry runtime with workspace cwd and session resume; agent-authored reply reaches Slack under customize identity
turn exceeding max runtime is killed, marked failed, retried per policy with backoff, attempts capped

token value absent from workspace files, repo tree, process argv, logs, AND the spawned turn's environment (runtime env dump probe) — turns reach Slack only through the swarm CLI/daemon and never hold transport tokens
outbound send path scans message text for credential-shaped strings (xoxb-/xapp-/sk_/bearer-like) and blocks the send on hit (defense-in-depth)

S4_lease_recovery.check: kill -9 daemon mid-claimed-job; after restart, expired lease returns job to claimable exactly once
recovered job re-run produces no duplicate outbound send (mark-sent) and no duplicate state row
writeback against a changed snapshot is rejected (existing guard still enforced through daemon path)

S5_presence.check: registry presence reflects daemon observation (online/in_turn/last_seen) and goes stale correctly when daemon stops
30s self-report worker loop and its launchd jobs removed; launchd carries exactly one swarm job (the daemon)

S6_curator_workload.check: curator workspace seeds pass the scrub gate (credentials=0, others' DM originals=0, owner-sensitive=0) before first turn
hourly reminder wakes curator; one turn claims <= batch-size jobs, annotates via bounded contract, writes back, posts one channel report
batch size, cadence, and max runtime enforced; a cycle exceeding limits stops itself and reports rather than continuing
backfill progresses monotonically across cycles with zero contract-violation rows (sampled conformance check)

S7_live_smoke.check: owner mentions an agent in Slack; agent replies with self-authored content under its own rendered identity
owner-visible conversation survives daemon kill/restart with no lost or duplicated replies
owner sees annotated real cards (summary + why-it-matters) on the board product after a scheduled curator cycle
owner declares the demo believable as colleagues-in-Slack; any 'that felt scripted' note reopens the relevant slice
